title: Cherry-Pick, Replay, Patch Equivalence, and Backports · Git Stakeholder Mission
summary: A multi-release library maintainer group. The group needs complex merge, rerere, rebase, and backport procedures that preserve provenance, semantic tests, review equivalence, and recovery across changed graphs. Planned artifact: Deliver Defensible Integration and Rewrite System with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.
Enable, inspect, forget, retrain, and validate rerere preimages and postimages while understanding automatic staging and retention.
Protect the old tip, inspect rewritten mapping, compare patch series and trees with range-diff, test changed behavior, and recover from abort or error.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — Case G49 is a project in a disposable repository. one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice. The learner must predict minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target, run git cherry -v release security-fix, explain the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work, test this changed condition: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior, and preserve this recovery boundary: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-backport-verification-108y4g" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Backport Verification into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-backport-verification-108y4g
# scenario-key: trace-108y4g probe-g4y801 boundary-8y4g10 owner-108y4gx fault-108y4gz
# before-ref: main=a11ce10 candidate=b00b14g
# hypothesis: one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice
git cherry -v release security-fix
# expected: minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target
# observation: the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work
# changed-case: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior
# after-ref: main=a11ce10 candidate=c0ffeeg
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations
# transfer-limit: patch equivalence is heuristic and each supported runtime needs changed-platform tests
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write the project evidence note for Gita Backport Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G57 is a project in a disposable repository. one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice. steps.why: Retrieving Gita Backport Verification through the concrete multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify source provenance, target applicability, conflicts, changed-platform behavior, release notes, signatures, and rollback for each supported line.
